claim_chain: Healthcare utilization in China was substantially disrupted during COVID-19 pandemic
Declines were most pronounced during initial waves but persisted into post-Zero-COVID period
Stringent Zero-COVID policies were strongly associated with reduced healthcare utilization
Despite gradual recovery, utilization remains below expected levels nationwide
Regional disparities highlight heterogeneity in healthcare disruptions
Findings have implications for long-term financial health of China's healthcare system
Strengthening healthcare systems needed to enhance resilience for future crises

typical_pattern: Opening with comprehensive summary of main finding
Quantitative evidence with specific numbers and percentages
Comparison with prior studies or other settings
Discussion of regional or subgroup variations

likely_priorities: Causal inference and disentangling of pathways
Quality of care assessment beyond utilization volumes
likely_attacks: Use of aggregated routine health system data limited individual-level analyses
Cannot disentangle causal pathways between policy and utilization
Assessment of care quality not possible with available data
Prolonged stringent Zero-COVID policy may limit comparability to other countries
Recovery period may be insufficient to assess full rebound
Financial strain on hospitals and health workers inadequately quantified
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly states 'Use of aggregated routine health system data limited individual-level analyses, assessment of care quality, and disentangling of causal pathways' as a limitation. The discussion acknowledges that 'China's prolonged and stringent Zero-COVID policy may have further delayed recovery compared to countries that adopted less restrictive measures', suggesting reviewers might question generalizability. The paper also notes concerns about 'whether their existing healthcare systems can meet local demand both in terms of quantity and quality', indicating awareness that quality assessment is missing. The discussion of financial implications mentions 'reports emerged of salary reductions for health workers' and 'Concerns have been raised that hospitals, facing financial strain, may have co...', suggesting incomplete data on economic impacts.

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. All numeric values appear in the original. No external references, author names, or invented data were included. The profile is based entirely on the provided abstract, discussion, and figure legends.
